summary: CR-6 Phase 2b /ai inline CMS G4 QA — ALL flows green. Verified: (1) admin login via ?admin=1 → editbar + 13 expected edit-ai.* pencils render (eyebrow, h1, sub, cta, features, crosslink.title/body/cta, faq_heading, faqs, demo.heading/sub/bullets). (2) Hero EditableText round-trip: edited ai.hero.h1 → saved → draft renders in admin → published → fresh logged-out tab sees new H1 ('QA Hero H1 Updated'). (3) Features EditableList with lockItems: ONE edit-ai.features pencil, slide-over has 7 rows, ZERO list-add buttons, ZERO Delete buttons, ZERO ↑/↓ buttons — fully locked. Dot-path fields (outcome.value, outcome.label) rendered with correct labels; videoSrc + poster each show file picker + paste-URL fallback input. (4) mergeFeatures correctness: edited ONLY menu-import (title='QA Smart Menu Import', outcome.value='+99% QA', outcome.label='QA outcome label') → other 6 features (customer-insights, smart-upsell, report-audit, ops-recommendations, smart-validations, crm-segmentation) kept their ORIGINAL titles after publish. lucide icon SVG still rendered next to feature #0 (code-controlled). (5) FAQ editor (new in Phase 2b): edit-ai.faqs opens faq-editor-ai.faqs slide-over with faq-add-ai.faqs + save-ai.faqs + per-row Q input, A textarea, details textarea, MediaBlock (type dropdown, file input, paste URL, caption), LinksBlock (label + Internal/External dropdown + URL + remove ×), ↑/↓ reorder, faq-delete-ai.faqs-<i>. Added 'QA FAQ' → saved → useSaveDoc wrote the full 6-item array (preserved-mode) under key=ai.faqs type=doc → publish → public list shows 6 FAQs with #5 = 'QA FAQ' rendered through FaqItem. (6) Logged-out regression on /ai: fallback H1, 7 original features (AI Menu Import, AI Customer Insights, AI Smart Upsell, AI Report Audit, AI Ops Recommendations, AI Smart Validations, AI CRM Segmentation), 5 original FAQs, no editbar, no edit-ai.* pencils, 2 JSON-LD scripts in head, hero CTA href='#ai-demo', FAQ #0 expands and shows answer. (7) /pricing regression intact after Phase 2b Editable.jsx changes: 4 plan cards (Starter ₹799 visible, Pro 'MOST POPULAR' badge present), 9 addon cards, no JS errors. (8) Home / and /blog smoke: both load with correct titles, no console errors. FINAL CLEANUP: discarded drafts via POST /api/cms/discard, then directly removed 3 ai.* docs (ai.features, ai.hero.h1, ai.faqs) from MongoDB cms_content collection. Re-verified GET /api/cms/content returns []; fresh logged-out /ai shows 100% fallback content (H1, 7 original feature titles, 5 original FAQs).

issues:
React hydration warning in console: '
cannot be a descendant of

'. Caused by EditableText wrapping ai.hero.sub (rendered with multiline+rich+block props) producing a

inside the hero subtitle

element at AiPage.jsx:130. Same pattern likely applies to crosslink.body and demo.sub which are also rendered with `block` inside

. Cosmetic only (no rendered breakage), but should be fixed in Editable wrapper or by switching the parent

to

when rich/multiline.

action_items:
(Low priority) Fix hydration warning: EditableText with multiline/rich/block renders a
wrapper that ends up inside

tags on /ai hero, crosslink body and demo sub. Either change Editable wrapper to when not in edit mode, or change parent

→

in AiPage.jsx for these three slots.
critical_code_review_comments: useContentDoc shallow-merge correctness verified: ai.hero.h1 alone was published as a string under key 'ai.hero.h1' (per-field key) while the per-page 'ai' doc was never written — the page-level useContentDoc('ai', fallback) returned the fallback and EditableText's per-key override path supplied the H1. This dual storage (per-key + per-doc) works, but the doc helper was effectively unused on this run. Consider whether you want both paths long-term or to consolidate on per-doc save like FAQs do via useSaveDoc.

FaqEditorModal opens with current items snapshot (5 fallback FAQs) and saves the FULL list — so adding 1 FAQ persisted as 6-item array (preserved-mode, not empty-replace). This is the desired default and is consistent with what the spec called 'original 5 FAQs PLUS the new one'.
mergeFeatures correctly preserves code-controlled fields (id, icon) and applies partial overrides per-id; null/undefined values fall back to base. Verified by editing only feature #0.
lockItems mode in shared ListModal correctly removes add/delete/reorder UI — same behavior as Phase 2a pricing.plans/pricing.addons. No regression.
Dot-path keys via getPath/setPath work in EditableList: 'outcome.value' / 'outcome.label' read and write to nested object. Verified end-to-end through the public render.
Image field in ListModal correctly accepts video uploads (accept attribute includes video/*) AND offers a paste-URL fallback input — verified visually in features modal for videoSrc + poster.
CmsProvider.uploadMedia: not exercised in this run (no actual file uploaded — only paste-URL path tested). Out of scope but worth a smoke test in a follow-up.

Hydration warning noted above is a real bug introduced by wrapping inside a

element — should be cleaned up but doesn't break functionality.

success_rate.backend: n/a (frontend-only requested; backend untouched in Phase 2b)

test_credentials: CMS admin: username=admin / password=admin123 (verified working at POST /api/cms/login via UI form data-testid=cms-username/cms-password/cms-login-submit).
seed_data_creation: During this run published 3 ai.* keys (ai.hero.h1='QA Hero H1 Updated', ai.features partial override on menu-import only, ai.faqs full 6-item array including 'QA FAQ' added at index 5). ALL CLEANED UP at end of run: POST /api/cms/discard + MongoDB cms_content.delete_many({key: /^ai\./}) removed 3 documents. Final state confirmed clean — GET /api/cms/content returns [] for ai.* and fresh logged-out /ai page shows 100% original fallback content (H1 'AI that works on the floor — not in a pitch deck.', 7 features with original titles, 5 original FAQs). The only docs left in cms_content are 5 pricing.* draft-only keys (no published_value) which do NOT appear in GET /api/cms/content — these are safely ignorable per the request's baseline list.
context_for_next_testing_agent:
Phase 2b /ai slice CLOSED green. Verified data-testids on /ai: ai-page, ai-hero, ai-hero-cta, ai-features, ai-feature- (7 ids), ai-central-inventory-link, ai-to-central-inventory, ai-faq, ai-faq-, ai-faq--toggle, ai-faq--answer, ai-demo-section. CMS testids confirmed: cms-username, cms-password, cms-login-submit, cms-editbar, cms-toggle-preview, cms-publish, cms-discard. Editable testids: edit-ai.hero.{eyebrow,h1,sub,cta}, edit-ai.features, edit-ai.crosslink.{title,body,cta}, edit-ai.faq_heading, edit-ai.faqs, edit-ai.demo.{heading,sub,bullets}, list-editor-ai.features, save-ai.features, faq-editor-ai.faqs, faq-add-ai.faqs, faq-q-ai.faqs-, faq-a-ai.faqs-, faq-delete-ai.faqs-, save-ai.faqs, field-ai.hero.h1, save-ai.hero.h1. lockItems confirmed on ai.features: list-add-ai.features ABSENT, 0 Delete/↑/↓ inside the slide-over. mergeFeatures preserves untouched items + code-controlled id/icon. Backend was NOT touched and remains 18/18 green from iteration_3. One cosmetic action item remains:
in

hydration warning from EditableText block-mode used inside hero/crosslink/demo

tags.
rca_of_the_issue:
Hydration warning RCA: AiPage.jsx wraps `` inside `

` elements at lines 130 (hero sub), 197 (crosslink body) and 251 (demo sub). When block=true the Editable Wrapper renders a `

` (with relative/group/cms classes), and a
is not a valid descendant of

. React strips/relocates the children at hydration, triggering the warning. Fixes (pick one): (a) drop `block` from those three EditableText calls — they don't need block layout for a paragraph; (b) change the Editable Wrapper to render a instead of a

when the consumer is text-only; (c) change the surrounding

to

with the same Tailwind classes. Recommend (a) as the lowest-risk change.
